message:
fix(ru-sweep): remove redundant JSON.stringify calls in sweep logging

When data or config objects are stored in JSONB columns, the ORM
handles serialization automatically. Calling JSON.stringify() before
insertion causes double-escaping: the string gets serialized again
when the row is inserted, resulting in escaped quotes within the
stored JSON.

Changes:
- logSweepEvent: Pass data directly using nullish coalescing (data ?? null)
  instead of JSON.stringify(data). The column type handles serialization.
- startAgentSweep: Pass config object directly instead of stringifying.
  The config JSONB column will serialize it properly.

This matches the fix in the previous commit (4ac0af1) for safety violations
and applies the same pattern to sweep session initialization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -256,7 +256,7 @@ async function logSweepEvent(
     phase: options?.phase || "sweep",
     level,
     message,
-    data: data ? JSON.stringify(data) : null,
+    data: data ?? null,
     timestamp: now,
     durationMs: options?.durationMs,
     actionType: options?.actionType,
@@ -361,7 +361,7 @@ export async function startAgentSweep(
     id: sessionId,
     targetRepos: JSON.stringify(repos.map((r) => r.id)),
     repoCount: repos.length,
-    config: JSON.stringify(config),
+    config,
     parallelism: config.parallelism || 1,
     status: "pending" as SweepStatus,
     reposAnalyzed: 0,
